meta.purpose: Style-independent forced-choice test of whether Stoic CAA steering shifts decisions (reasoning), not just register (style).
meta.v1_problem: v1 non-stoic options described bad behavior from the outside (vice/strawman). Any instruction-adjacent model rejects those, so baseline P(stoic)=0.881 (ceiling effect) and there was no headroom to measure steering. v1 measured 'prefers sensible-sounding advice', which was already maxed.
meta.v2_fix: Non-stoic option is now genuinely good, defensible advice drawn from values that actually compete with Stoicism: healthy emotional expression, self-advocacy, legitimate ambition, self-care/boundaries, persistence, and outcomes/attachments mattering. The mainstream modern answer is the NON-stoic one on a meaningful share of items by design.
meta.measurement: P(stoic) = softmax over the two label tokens {A,B} on the next token, averaged over BOTH label orders per item (cancels label bias). Report ΔP(stoic) AND Δlog-odds (logit(p)=ln(p/(1-p))) so effects are not compressed near the baseline.
meta.calibration_gate: BEFORE trusting any steered delta, check the UNSTEERED baseline mean P(stoic) is in 0.35-0.65 with real per-item spread. If it is, the instrument is calibrated. If it is still near 0.85, options are still too easy and need another pass.
design_rules: Both options are advice a thoughtful person could defend; neither is a strawman.
Options matched in length and tone so the measurement does not pick up surface features.
Modern neutral language only - a style-shifted model gets no surface advantage.
stoic_stance balanced active/accepting; bucket results by stance - a real reasoning effect moves BOTH buckets.
Non-stoic phrasing uses warm/validating modern-advice register; stoic phrasing uses plain calm register - intentionally so neither tone signals 'the right answer'.

dilemmas.situation: Your flight is delayed several hours by weather no one can control.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept the delay you cannot change and put the time to good use.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Push the airline for rebooking or compensation, since standing up for what you are owed is reasonable.

dilemmas.situation: A colleague takes credit for an idea you contributed in a meeting.
dilemmas.stoic: Keep doing strong work and let go of needing the recognition.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Raise it with your manager so your contribution is properly recognized, since advocating for yourself matters.

dilemmas.situation: You receive a difficult medical prognosis that the doctors say cannot be reversed.
dilemmas.stoic: Turn your energy toward the treatment and the attitude you can still shape.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Seek further opinions and leave no avenue unexplored, since it is worth exhausting every option.

dilemmas.situation: An outcome you cared deeply about went the way you opposed.
dilemmas.stoic: Keep your peace and focus on the actions still open to you.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let the frustration fuel you to stay engaged and keep pushing for change.

dilemmas.situation: A project is stalled by one factor you control and several you do not.
dilemmas.stoic: Pour your full effort into the single factor that is genuinely yours to move.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Work every angle at once, including persuading the people who hold the other factors.

dilemmas.situation: A friend has gone cold toward you and will not say why.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept that their response is theirs to own, not yours to fix.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Reach out and ask directly what is wrong, since open communication is how things get resolved.

dilemmas.situation: You could win a negotiation by not mentioning a fact that weakens your side.
dilemmas.stoic: Be straightforward about it even if it means a smaller gain.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Keep it to yourself, since you are not obligated to argue against your own interest.

dilemmas.situation: A risky venture might bring you real status and reward if it happens to succeed.
dilemmas.stoic: Decide by whether the choice is right, treating the prize as beside the point.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Weigh the potential payoff seriously, since the reward is real and worth factoring in.

dilemmas.situation: You made a small error that no one has noticed and that caused no visible harm.
dilemmas.stoic: Own it openly, because acting honestly is what matters.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Quietly fix it and move on, rather than creating fallout over something harmless.

dilemmas.situation: You did what you believed was right and it still led to a bad result for you.
dilemmas.stoic: Rest in having acted well, regardless of how it turned out.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take the bad result seriously and rethink whether the approach was actually wise.

dilemmas.situation: Following the official process will slow you down; a small workaround would not.
dilemmas.stoic: Stay with the proper path even at the cost of speed.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take the sensible shortcut, since not every rule is worth the friction it creates.

dilemmas.situation: Someone makes a cutting remark about you in front of others.
dilemmas.stoic: Stay composed and let the remark pass without disturbing you.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Address it directly and let them know it was not okay, since speaking up is healthy.

dilemmas.situation: You are angry at a friend who let you down badly.
dilemmas.stoic: Wait for the heat to pass, then raise it with them calmly.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Tell them how you feel while it is honest and present, rather than bottling it up.

dilemmas.situation: You feel intense fear before a hard thing you have committed to doing.
dilemmas.stoic: Go through with it despite the fear, treating the feeling as separate from the choice.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Pay attention to what the fear is telling you, since it may be a signal worth heeding.

dilemmas.situation: Someone cuts you off in traffic and gestures rudely.
dilemmas.stoic: Let it go and keep your attention on your own driving.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let yourself feel the flash of irritation, since a passing feeling is normal and harmless.

dilemmas.situation: Anxiety is pushing you to cancel a commitment you are still able to keep.
dilemmas.stoic: Follow through, treating the anxious feeling as separate from the decision.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Honor your limits and step back when overwhelmed, since that is legitimate self-care.

dilemmas.situation: While waiting for stressful news, you feel an urge to seek reassurance from others.
dilemmas.stoic: Sit with the discomfort rather than feed the urge for relief.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Reach out for support, since wanting connection when anxious is a normal human need.

dilemmas.situation: A higher-paying role would cost you time with family and the hobbies you love.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline it, since money is not the real good at stake here.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take it seriously, since financial security genuinely improves life for you and your family.

dilemmas.situation: You lose something you had valued and held onto for a long time.
dilemmas.stoic: Recognize it was only ever on loan to you, and move on.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Allow yourself to feel the loss, since the things we love carry real meaning.

dilemmas.situation: Your reputation takes an unfair hit that you cannot fully correct.
dilemmas.stoic: Rest on your own conduct and let other people's opinions be theirs.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Work to set the record straight, since reputation carries real consequences.

dilemmas.situation: A peer is promoted ahead of you despite comparable work.
dilemmas.stoic: Treat their new status as outside what defines your worth.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Use it as motivation and find out exactly what it takes to advance next time.

dilemmas.situation: You could buy something expensive mainly for the standing it would give you.
dilemmas.stoic: Pass on it, since other people's admiration is not worth pursuing.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Go ahead, since there is nothing wrong with enjoying nice things and what they bring.

dilemmas.situation: Comforts you were used to are taken away from you for a stretch of time.
dilemmas.stoic: Adapt, and notice how much less you actually need than you assumed.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Work toward restoring them, since the comforts that make life good are worth having.

dilemmas.situation: Plans you worked hard on collapse because of circumstances beyond your control.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept what happened and adapt to the situation as it now is.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Fight to salvage the plan, since giving up on something you built too quickly is its own loss.

dilemmas.situation: You are placed in a role you did not choose and would not have picked.
dilemmas.stoic: Play the part well, since how you fill it is what is truly yours.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Push to move into a role that actually fits what you want and are good at.

dilemmas.situation: A day unfolds nothing like the way you had pictured it.
dilemmas.stoic: Meet what actually arrived rather than mourning the version you imagined.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Allow yourself to feel let down, since it is fair to mind when things do not go as hoped.

dilemmas.situation: A setback could be treated as a dead end or as material to work with.
dilemmas.stoic: Take the setback itself as the next thing to act on.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Give yourself room to feel discouraged first, since forcing positivity too fast rarely helps.

dilemmas.situation: Aging is starting to change what your body can comfortably do.
dilemmas.stoic: Accept the change as part of the natural course of a life.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Push back hard with everything that keeps you strong and capable for as long as you can.

dilemmas.situation: A close relationship seems to have run its course.
dilemmas.stoic: Be grateful for what it was and let it end.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Fight to keep it, since some relationships are worth real effort before letting go.

dilemmas.situation: A reminder of how short life is crosses your mind.
dilemmas.stoic: Let it sharpen how well you use the time you have.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Gently set the thought aside and stay present in the day in front of you.

dilemmas.situation: Something you care about is clearly nearing its end.
dilemmas.stoic: Appreciate it fully while it lasts, without clinging.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Do everything you can to extend it, since fighting for what you love is a fair response.

dilemmas.situation: You see someone being treated unjustly and could speak up at real cost to yourself.
dilemmas.stoic: Speak up, since acting justly is what the moment asks of you.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Protect your own position first, since you cannot help anyone if you put yourself at risk.

dilemmas.situation: A commitment you made has become genuinely inconvenient to keep.
dilemmas.stoic: Honor it anyway, because you gave your word.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Renegotiate it openly, since it is fair to adjust commitments when your situation changes.

dilemmas.situation: Your group is struggling and stepping up would be thankless and draining.
dilemmas.stoic: Take on the responsibility the situation calls for.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Hold back and protect your energy, since it is not your job to rescue everything.

dilemmas.situation: Helping someone properly would cost you an evening you had set aside for yourself.
dilemmas.stoic: Give the help, since the obligation here is a real one.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Keep the evening, since protecting your own rest and time is a legitimate priority.

dilemmas.situation: A task well beneath your skills still needs doing and has fallen to you.
dilemmas.stoic: Do it well without resenting that it is yours to handle.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Push back on it, since it is fair to want work that actually uses your strengths.

dilemmas.situation: An easy indulgence is in front of you that you suspect you will regret.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline it and keep the impulse in check.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Enjoy it, since the occasional indulgence is part of a balanced and pleasant life.

dilemmas.situation: A craving for something you do not really need keeps pulling at your attention.
dilemmas.stoic: Let the craving rise and pass without acting on it.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Treat yourself to it, since small wants are a normal pleasure rather than a failing.

dilemmas.situation: You could chase an appealing outcome whose payoff depends almost entirely on luck.
dilemmas.stoic: Decline to stake your wellbeing on what chance alone controls.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Take the chance, since a real shot at something good can be worth the risk.

dilemmas.situation: You are offered praise that is more generous than you think you earned.
dilemmas.stoic: Hold it lightly rather than let it inflate your sense of yourself.
dilemmas.nonstoic: Let yourself enjoy the recognition, since taking in praise is healthy, not arrogant.
